# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our Python Flask API. The app needs to install dependencies from requirements.txt, expose port 5000, and run the Flask application. Keep it simple and straightforward for quick deployment to production.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image
FROM python:3-slim

# Set environment variables 
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED True

# Install system dependencies (e.g., libpq, needed for Flask)
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y build-essential curl git python3 psql postgresql && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* 

# Set working directory in the container to a location where your app will be mounted, and create an requirements file for our application.  
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy local code to Python Flask App folder 
COPY ./src/flask_api/. /app/src/flask_api/

# Make port available for the world outside this container, and expose it on a specific host interface (default is all interfaces)  
EXPOSE 5000

CMD ["python", "./src/flask_api/run.py"]
